critic_raw:
This multimodal sentiment analysis design for customer satisfaction prediction has a temporal leakage problem and a comparison scope issue.

First, the design uses stratified random split across all 24 months 'without constraints on temporal ordering.' For e-commerce review data, temporal ordering matters: product quality trends, seasonal purchasing patterns, and user demographic shifts evolve over time. A random split puts reviews from month 24 in training while month 1 reviews appear in test — the model may learn patterns from future promotional periods or product trends that did not exist during earlier test periods. The design explicitly states no temporal constraints, accepting this leakage.

Second, Section 3 states preprocessing transformers 'are fit on the entire dataset before splitting.' This is the standard leakage issue seen throughout this review — normalization statistics, BERT tokenization, and image normalization all incorporate test-set distributional information.

Third, the baseline model (text only) uses 'the identical architecture but without image and metadata branches.' However, the metadata branch includes product category, price tier, promotional flag, and seasonal encoding. The baseline excludes metadata as well as images. The comparison tests 'text vs. text + images + metadata,' not 'text vs. text + images.' Any observed advantage for the multimodal model could be due to metadata features (especially promotional flag and category) rather than image features. A proper ablation would require comparing text-only vs. text+metadata vs. text+images vs. text+images+metadata.

Fourth, Section 6 refers to 'the validation set (months 17–20).' This is inconsistent with the stated random split strategy in Section 2 — if the split is random without temporal constraints, there is no 'months 17–20' validation set. This is the same split contradiction seen in multiple other designs.
all_issues_raised: Temporal leakage: random split across 24 months without temporal ordering constraints
Preprocessing leakage: transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting
Confounded comparison: baseline excludes metadata AND images — cannot isolate image contribution
Split contradiction: Section 6 references months 17-20 validation set inconsistent with random split

round_transcripts.critic: This design has temporal leakage from random split, preprocessing leakage, a confounded comparison that excludes metadata as well as images (preventing isolation of image contribution), and a split contradiction between the random split described in Section 2 and the month-defined validation set referenced in Section 6.
round_transcripts.defender:
On temporal leakage: For e-commerce reviews, temporal patterns (seasonal trends, promotional effects) are real. However, the design includes promotional flag and month as explicit features, which partially controls for temporal confounds by making them observable to the model rather than hidden. Whether a temporal split is strictly required depends on whether the deployment use case involves out-of-temporal-distribution generalization. The design should clarify its deployment scenario.

On preprocessing leakage: Conceded. Fitting BERT tokenization and image normalization on the entire dataset before splitting is a data leakage error.

On confounded comparison: The critic is correct that the baseline excludes both images and metadata. If metadata features (promotions, category) drive satisfaction prediction more than images, the multimodal advantage is confounded. A text+metadata baseline would isolate the image contribution. The defender concedes this is a genuine ablation gap.

On split contradiction: Section 2 describes random split without temporal ordering; Section 6 specifies 'months 17–20 validation set.' These are incompatible. The defender concedes this is the same split specification ambiguity seen in other designs.

round_transcripts.critic: Three confirmed issues: preprocessing leakage, confounded comparison (missing text+metadata baseline), and split contradiction. The temporal leakage concern is partially mitigated by explicit temporal features but not eliminated.
round_transcripts.defender: Three concessions confirmed. The experiment cannot isolate image contribution from metadata contribution; the split specification is internally contradictory; preprocessing leakage is confirmed.

all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing leakage: transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting
Confounded comparison: baseline excludes metadata AND images — image contribution cannot be isolated
Split contradiction: random split claim vs. month-defined validation set in Section 6
